Errors that surface after such a run need to be checked and resolved manually, which can be time consuming. This is where the new payment exception analysis comes in handy: it helps accounts payable (AP) accountants to identify the root cause of the error, giving them the context,  and a step-by-step guide for resolving the issue. Figure 13 below shows an example.

Figure 13: Beta: AI-Assisted Payment Exception Analysis. Alt Text: Screenshot of the Revise Payment Proposals app with an Exception Analysis and Resolution pop-over, with sections “Analysis”, “Resolution” and “Logs”.

 Watch it in action and find out more:

  • Video: AI-Assisted Payment Exception Analysis (1:21 minutes)
  • SAP Discovery Center: Payment Exception Analysis.

 

Application-Specific Embedded AI Use Cases

Beyond dealing with errors and exceptions, we also have a number of interesting cases available for beta testing where AI helps users get their work done directly within their application. I have chosen to feature just one of these, but after that you will find links to more examples.

Beta: EHS Permit Management

This new feature also has huge potential for saving effort and cost: we estimate 65% cost reduction in environmental permit management cost, and 80% reduction in environmental management penalties and fines. In other words: a huge improvement in user experience and user satisfaction!

Environmental, health and safety regulations can range from a few pages to hundreds of pages, depending on what it is regulating. Checking these documents and ensuring that your company is compliant, with all the necessary tasks in place to stay compliant, is a tedious and time-consuming task.

This new permit management feature automatically extracts and sets up an EHS- permit from PDF documents. It identifies all the requirements and makes proposals for the necessary tasks, ranging from simple ones to maintenance notification tasks and reporting tasks. The user can flip through the regulatory document in the left side of the screen. For each page that they look at, they see highlighted the part of the document containing a concrete requirement, and on the right side of the screen a list of proposed tasks. With one click, the user can select a proposed task and create an actual task. Figure 14 shows an example.

Figure 14: AI-Assisted Permit Management for SAP EHS Management, environment  management. Alt Text: Screenshot showing one page of a PDF document on the left with a paragraph higlighted, and a list of proposed tasks on the right.

Have a look at this video to see it in action, in particular how users can select from the proposed tasks to create tasks:

  • Video: EHS Permit Management.
  • SAP Discovery Center: EHS Permit Management.

Additional Use Cases for Embedded AI in Beta

Here are three more examples for AI embedded in applications, helping users get their work done more easily.

Beta: AI-Assisted Compliance Information Processing

With this innovation, master data specialists can automatically process supplier compliance disclosures information such as regulations, status and upstream suppliers data, and map it to compliance requirements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for product compliance.

  • Video (1:28 minutes) and SAP Discovery Center entry.

Beta: AI-Assisted Safety Instruction Generation

This innovation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, EHS workplace safety, automatically generates clear, structured and easy-to-understand safety instructions for specific equipment.

  • Video (1:35 minutes) and SAP Discovery Center entry.

 

Continue reading

I hope you enjoyed this third part, covering the new UX innovations available for beta testing with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602. Here's a link to part four, covering exciting new UX innovations available in SAP Success Factors:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 4: SAP SuccessFactors (AI, Joule and More).

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: Asset Management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to the blog post for asset management in SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release! This release introduces significant innovations in maintenance management, designed to enhance productivity for maintenance experts, improve safety and compliance protocols, and provide more actionable, centralized data.

Below, you’ll find a curated selection of release highlights from our core product management team. Before diving in, I invite you to watch the release highlights video, featuring a digital version of me! 😀

 

Table of Contents

New Asset Overview SAP Fiori app (+demo) 
Permit to work: new scope item (7Z4) (+demo) 
Enhancements to the Manage Maintenance Notification app (+demo) 
Further innovations 
SAP Asset Performance Management 
More information/additional links

New Asset Overview SAP Fiori app

To address the challenge of navigating multiple screens to gather asset information, the 2602 release introduces the Asset Overview app. This SAP Fiori application provides a single, unified interface for maintenance experts to view and interact with the complete hierarchy of their enterprise assets. Access to this application requires EAM - Asset Overview (SAP_EAM_BC_AOVW_MNG_PC) business catalog, which is included in role templates such as Maintenance Planner (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_PLANNER), Maintenance Technician (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_TECHNICIAN), and Maintenance Supervisor (SAP_BR_MAINTENANCE_SUPERVISOR).

Key features and capabilities:

Unified hierarchy visualization: The app offers a comprehensive top-down and bottom-up view of your asset structure, combining functional locations and the equipment installed within them into a single, traversable tree. Users can expand individual nodes or the entire hierarchy to its lowest level with a single click. A powerful search function allows users to isolate a specific asset and display its complete branch up to the top-level parent.

The new app aims to provide asset-centric data access, focused on:

  • Flexible column layout: This feature allows a user to select an asset in the hierarchy on the left-hand panel and view its detailed information on the right-hand panel without leaving the main screen.
  • Master and transactional data: The detail view displays crucial master data, such as manufacturer and model number, alongside visual indicators for warranty status (e.g., color-coded for active, expired or not maintained). It also provides direct access to related transactional data, including open maintenance notifications and orders associated with the asset.

Integrated maintenance actions: The app is not just for viewing data; it enables immediate action. Users can create maintenance notifications directly from the asset detail page through two methods:

  • Quick create: A simple form requiring a description to generate a notification.
  • Joule, SAP’s AI Copilot: Users can leverage the integrated digital assistant, Joule, to create a notification using natural language prompts without leaving the app.

Learn more watching the demo below:

 

Permit to work: new scope item (7Z4)

The 2602 release introduces the Permit to Work functionality under scope item 7Z4. This feature provides a standardized process for issuing permits for high-risk maintenance activities, ensuring work is performed in a safe, secure, and compliant manner. It is particularly critical for industries such as manufacturing, oil and gas, mining, and utilities.

At its core, the Permit to Work process serves as a formal authorization system. It's designed to foster close collaboration between your maintenance, operations, and safety teams, ensuring everyone is aligned on the required precautions to minimize risks.

Initially, this scope item is available for Germany and the US. Availability for other countries will follow in subsequent updates, with a target for all standard countries by the 2702 release.

Prerequisite scope items include Reactive Maintenance (4HH), Proactive Maintenance (4H), Operational and Overhead Maintenance (4BT), and Maintenance Resource Scheduling (4WM).

Core components and configuration

New applications: This functionality is delivered with two new SAP Fiori apps:

  • Manage Work Permit Template (F4692): Allows for the creation of reusable templates with pre-defined information. This reduces the time required to create new permits and ensures that minimum safety and data standards are consistently met.
  • Manage Work Permits (F6579): The primary application for creating, processing, and managing the lifecycle of individual work permits. Permits can be created with reference to a maintenance order, an existing permit, or a pre-configured template.

Process overview

1. Initiation: A planner identifies a maintenance activity that requires a permit and initiates its creation, selecting a permit type such as hot work, cold work, or confined space.
2. Approval: The permit is submitted for review. In the initial 2602 release, the permit is automatically set to a "Ready for Issue" status. Flexible workflow capabilities are planned for future releases.
3. Issuance: An authorized user, such as a supervisor, issues the permit to the designated technician.
4. Execution and return: The technician completes the work and returns the permit to the issuer.
5. Closure: The issuer verifies the work site, normalizes the technical object, and formally closes the permit in the system. The system supports actions to "retain and close" or "retain and deactivate" the permit if work needs to be suspended and resumed later.

 Learn more watching the demo below by Ebin Joseph, from product management:

 

Enhancements to the Manage Maintenance Notification app

The Manage Maintenance Notification (F5777) app has been updated with several new features to improve usability and provide deeper insights.

Situation handling: The app now uses situation handling to deliver instant alerts to the maintenance team when a new notification is created or a key status is updated. For example, an alert can be triggered when a notification is automatically generated due to a condition-based maintenance threshold breach, enabling a proactive response.

Advanced filtering: The list report now includes "Asset Location" and "Sort Field" as filter criteria. This allows users to more effectively narrow down the list of notifications to the specific assets or areas for which they are responsible.

Visual charts for measurement documents: For notifications generated from a measurement document threshold breach, the header now includes a chart. This provides a clear visual representation of the measurement readings against their defined thresholds, allowing for quicker analysis.

Reference order tracking: A "Reference Order" field has been added as a filter and a column in the list report. This enables maintenance teams to easily identify and track all notifications that were created as a follow-on work request from a technician in the field.

Conversational AI: Similar to the Asset Overview app, users can now create maintenance notifications directly within this app using natural language conversations with Joule AI Copilot.

 Learn more watching the demo below by Syed Kader Abdul Wahab, from product management:

 

Further innovations

Perform Maintenance Jobs app - Long text formatting: Clarity in work instructions is critical for maintenance technicians. Long text fields for maintenance orders and jobs can now be formatted. This enhancement allows for the use of bolding, bullet points, and other formatting options to structure text, which improves the readability and organization of detailed instructions and notes.

Manage Maintenance Plans app - Deletion of sort fields: To improve data quality and efficiency, the Define Sort Fields for Maintenance Plans configuration activity now includes a delete option. You can now remove outdated or invalid customized sort field values. This ensures that only accurate and relevant sort field values are available in the Maintenance Plan application, improving accuracy in plan management.

Centralized processing of maintenance order workflows in SAP Task Center: Efficiency in approval processes is a key driver of productivity. Maintenance Order workflow attributes are now accessible in SAP Task Center, allowing for centralized processing of approvals. The following attributes are included: Order Description, Order Type, Planner Group, Planning Plant, Basic End Date, Basic Start Date, Priority, and Technical Object. This centralization helps managers and approvers process tasks from a single, unified inbox.

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management integrates with SAP Cloud ERP and empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives. The 2601 release highlights include bulk activation and deactivation of system elements, more flexible risk and criticality assessment outcomes, CAP-based reliability assessment applications with extensibility, configurable RCM consequence questionnaires, validity periods for assessments, time-window–based rule processing, and embedded analytics dashboards. Read the complete blog post by Kate Dowle: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2601.

If you’d like to stay up to date with the latest APM innovations, follow my colleague Kate Dowle who regularly shares updates and new blog posts on the topic.
 

More information / additional links 

Find all enablement assets and update blog post for Asset Management: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Also, visit the Asset Management community page. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow me on LinkedIn and stay up-to-date to our latest enablement assets: Fernanda Rodrigues | LinkedIn

 

 Find all our assets

Do you want to learn more about the main innovations in different lines of business in the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release? The release 2602 showcases more 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

Enablement

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you: 

 

 ️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series!

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Asset Management as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime.

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter February 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here a preview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.

entry.title: Converting to the 3-System Landscape – Part 4: How SAP Supports Your Transition
entry.content:
Co-Author: Dennis Leslie

Still in doubt if you should follow the path to convert your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition landscape to the new 3-system landscape?

In the video linked below, Tom Konings highlights the key benefits relevant for dsm-firmenich’s i-Health division to plan and perform the conversion in a remarkable 23 days. He also explains that they can now improve and adapt faster to changing business requirements and are much more in control without any disruptions.

In addition, Dennis Leslie and Calin Cernea from SAP explain why and how SAP supports customers to convert to the 3-system landscape with the conversion program. To help you transition seamlessly, the SAP Activate roadmap provides structured guidance and clear instructions, paired with engaging enablement materials. While good project planning and change management are crucial for a successful project, SAP also provides additional tools like the Cloud Integration Automation service and the test data conversion capability, helping customers to perform a smooth conversion.

Expert talk with HCLTech on YouTube (Part 2)

Explore the power of the 3-system landscape today! To learn more, check out the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Three-System Landscape page on SAP Community.

Missed the earlier posts in this series? No worries, you can catch up on all of them here:

entry.title: Custom Fields in SAP S/4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Introduction

In Key User Extensibility,  Custom fields can be added using Custom Fields App.

Custom Fields in SAP S/4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD

Custom Fields is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools                        

In the Custom Fields App, 

  • Create and edit custom fields (extend the SAP table, application, CDS views and OData services)
  • Delete an existing custom field
  • Manage the usages of a custom field such as other Uls, reports, forms, service interfaces, processes, search
  • Define fields of type Text, number, amount, quantity, time, date, timestamp, ...
  • Value help views based on fixed code list or based on CDS view.

Steps to Create Custom Fields

Navigation to Custom Fields App

Custom Fields App

Click on Create

Choose Required Business Context

Add Label name (Identifier, Tooltip appears automatically based on given label name).

Choose Type of a Custom Fields.

Here Phone Number is chosen, System automatically assigns length of it.

If Text Type is chosen, then manually length should be given.

Based on the length of a field, business context capacity will be increased.

Available Types for the field

Choose Create or Create and Edit options

Choose APIS

To expose custom fields through APIs, ensure the necessary APIs are enabled so the fields become accessible.

To display custom fields in the SAP GUI, make sure the relevant data sources are enabled so the fields appear on the screen

Enable the SAP GUI

To expose custom fields through services, ensure the necessary service are enabled so the fields become accessible.

To make custom fields available in form templates, enable the appropriate templates to ensure the fields are accessible

General Information provides information of the custom fields with all label texts

Different Labels can be maintained for a custom field.

Custom field is added in the Purchase Order Header Screen.

Extended CDS View of Custom Field

After Creating Custom field and enabling related CDS View or API, that CDS View will be extended with that custom field.

For every custom field, separate Extended view will be generated.

Select the Extended view, it shows the custom field.

The custom field is added in the Extended view of Purchase Order CDS View

References

  • Before Getting Started with Custom Fields | SAP Help Portal
  • Creating Custom Fields | SAP Help Portal
  • Key User Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Extend and Integrate Your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal

Conclusion

Using Custom Fields App,

Custom fields can be added to the UI.

Create and edit custom fields and Delete an existing custom field.

Value help views can be added to the custom fields.

Custom fields can also be added to the custom forms.

entry.title: TCode也能快速启动了
entry.content:
在之前介绍2602版本新功能的博客里，我提到了被广大SAP用户熟知的/n和/o的快捷键指令也能在ERP公有云版本中使用了。比如我在搜索框中输入/nf2450，就会打开f2450对应的应用：IAM信息系统：

 

 

         之前，通过这样的方式打开应用只支持Fiori的应用，不支持WebGui版本的应用。但被广大SAP用户所熟知的都是以前那些常用的TCode，比如VA01，VA02，VA03，MM01，MIGO等等。

         现在，我们这个快速启动应用的功能做了改进，也支持WebGui的应用了，也就是那些常用的TCode也能快速启动了！

         比如，我在搜索框中输入/nVA03：

 

         系统就会在当前页面打开“显示销售凭证”的WebGUI版本的应用：

 

         再比如，我们常用的TCode：MIGO，当我输入/nMIGO后：

 

系统就在当前页面打开了我们最常用的MIGO应用：

 

快速总结一下：

  • 搜索框中/n和/o的快捷指令从2602版本开始支持。
  • 除了支持Fiori ID外，以前的TCode（如果有相应的WebGUI应用）也支持了，这对于SAP ERP的老用户非常友好，进而提升工作效率。

entry.title: Custom Logic, BAdi Enhancement Implementation - On Stack Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

In OnStack extensibility, Enhancements can be created using Custom Logic App in Key User Extensibility and BAdi Enhancement Implementation in Developer Extensibility.

Key user extensibility also know as In App extensibility, extensions can be added with key user tools.

Developer extensibility using sap cloud abap environment for developer extensions.

Key User Extensibility / In App Extensibility- Custom Logic

Custom Logic is one of the Key User Extensibility Tools                        

In the Custom logic App, 

  • Custom logic can be added
  • Easy exploration of extension points, key user documentation
  • Syntax check, syntax highlighting, code completion
  • Draft, published, testing included
  • ABAP Language Version for Key Users
  • Enhancements can be created using Custom Logi App

Business Accelerator Hub

SAP Business Accelerator Hub provides a section for On Stack extensibility (key user extensibility and developer extensibility), to know list of SAP CDS views, APIs, Business Add-ins etc. are available with the release status.

Navigate to Business Accelerator Hub to find BAdis

On Stack Extensibility -> Business Add-ins

Search for required BAdi.

This BAdi is related to check purchase order before saving to create it.

Here it shows BAdi Definition and Enhancement spot.

BAdi can be used in both Key User and Developer Extensibility as both states of BAdi is in released state.

Methods and its Parameters

Steps to Create Custom Logic

Navigate to Custom logic App

Custom Logic App

Choose Create button to create Custom Logic.

 

Choose Required Business Content

 

List of available BAdi Definitions for the business context.

Choose required BAdi Definition.

Its Documentation.

Choose Step 2 to add Implementation Description and ID.

Implementation ID automatically appears based on Description and it can also be changed.

Choose Review

Choose Create

Click on Publish button.

Its published.

Click on Open Code Editor to write the logic.

Importing Parameters.

Changing Parameters.

Sampe Code -> Show Sample Code

Keyboard Shortcuts -> three dot menu -> Show Keyboard Shortcuts

Write the required logic.

 Navigate to Create Purchase order (ME21N)

Provide the required details and check.

Error is appeared as per logic written.

This implementation class can be opened in the eclipse, and break point can be added in the logic, and it triggers when creating purchase order in the Create Purchase Order (ME21N).

Key-user objects can be displayed and debugged but cannot be edited with ABAP development tools.

Developer Extensibility – BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Steps to Create Impelementation

Create New BAdi Enhancement Implementation

Chose BAdi Enhancement Implementation.

Add Name and description.

Add Enhancement Spot

BAdi Enhancement Implementation is created.

Choose BAdi Implementation to add Implementations.

Add BAdi Definition and Custom Class name

Add Implementation Class

Choose Implementation Class, add Custom Class name and its description

Implementation Class is created.

Implementation Class is added to Badi Implementation details

Logic can be added to implementation class

Navigate to Create Purchase Order (ME21N)

Provide the required details and check

Error appeared as per logic written.

Add breakpoint in the implementation class to debug.

Place cursor on the logic, choose right click.

Break point is added

Break point can be added in the logic, and it triggers when creating purchase order in the Create Purchase Order (ME21N).

References

  • Custom Logic | SAP Help Portal
  • Custom Logic Tracing | SAP Help Portal
  • Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Key User Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Developer Extensibility | SAP Help Portal
  • Using Key-User In-App Extensibility to Create Custom Business Log
  • Implementing BAdI Enhancement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal

Conclusion                     

In Key User Extensibility, Custom logic can be added and Enhancements can be created using Custom Logic App.

In Developer Extensibility, BAdi Enhancement Implementation can be created.

entry.title: Transport Mechanism of Key-User Extensibility Objects in Public Cloud & Private Cloud
entry.content:
We are now quite familiar with the concept of Key User Extensibility/In-App Extensibility while working in a S/4 HANA Cloud environment. Key User Extensibility is available in both the environments of S/4 HANA Cloud (Public Edition & Private Edition) and is the preferred mode of creating extension due to its highest Clean Core level and being lightweight.

There are many blogposts available in the community showing creation and usage of the most commonly used Key User Extensibility Objects like Custom Fields / Custom Business Objects / Custom CDS Views etc.

However, in this blog post we will discuss about the difference in approach to Transport the Key User Extensibility objects to higher environments (Dev -> Test/QA ->Prod) in S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition & S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition .

Considering a use case of  Custom fields for demonstration. Although for creation and modification of custom fields, the approach is similar in both Private and Public cloud but there are few differences in approach while it comes to transporting them and one of the key differences being -

A transport process for Key User Extensibility objects in Private Cloud are  associated with an actual Transport Request number while in Public Cloud there is no actual Transport Request number associated while transporting them.

In this blogpost we will focus on the Transportation part but however for explanation I have added brief details on the creation process too.

S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition

S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition

Creation -

Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

 

Create the new custom field for the required business context and activate it for the required areas where you want to use this custom field -

 



 

After Enabling Usage for the required areas, click on Publish for the field to be available on the selected contexts.

 

If you want to add this field to your Fiori Application’s UI then you can do that using the option Adapt UI from the particular Fiori application for which you have created and exposed the custom field.

 

 

*Same as Public Cloud in this context
Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

The steps for activating the usage of the custom field and publishing are also same in S/4 HANA Private Cloud as shown in the other column for Public Cloud so you can follow all the same screenshots given in the left column for Private Cloud environment also.

**Screenshots have not been repeated.

Transport process -

No actual Transport Request number associated. Transport of Key User Extensibility objects happens using two Fiori Applications –

 Export Software Collection (To be used in Source System)

 Import Collection (To be used in Target system)

 

Transport happens with actual Transport Request number with two Fiori applications used –

 Configure Software Packages

 Register Extensions for Transport

Transportation process in Source system -

The transportation process is done by Software Collection.

The first step is to create a Software Collection and add the Custom field in the collection. This is done by the Fiori App – Export Software Collection

 

Once you create a Software collection you have to add your Custom field or whichever Extensibility object you are planning to move by clicking on Add Items

 

 

You can filter your Objects by selecting the type of objects you want to add in the Software Collection

 

 

Once everything is added you can click on Check and then finally Export

 

First step go to the Fiori Application Configure Software Packages and add registration for your package by clicking on the button Add Registration

 

 

Add your Transport Number from the F4 search help available under the column Transport Request to assign the package to a particular Transport number available.
Optionally you can turn on the Automatic Request handling for this package with the option available.

 

 

Second step go to the Fiori Application Register Extensions For Transport

 

Select your Custom field or the Extensibility object that you want to transport and click on Reassign to Package and select the package you registered in previous step.

 

 

 

By default your Extensibility object will get assigned to the Transport to which the package is assigned but you can change the transport assignment by clicking on the option Assign to Transport Request.

You can now go to Transaction Code SE10 and check the transport number under your name. The Custom field or the  extensibility object that you added will be available in the Transport Request .

You can release and move the transport to upper environments based on the transport mechanism that is being followed in your project.

Transportation process in Target system -

Based on architecture if it is a 3-Tier Public Cloud system then the next higher environment is going to be the Test Environment in Public Cloud.

In the Test Environment open the application Import Collection

 

 

 

Select your Software collection that you exported from source system and click on Check and Import

 

 

If you want to move your Software Collection to production environment then you can move it by the Forward option in the Import Collection app

 

No such similar activity is required here. Transport will be moved and available in QA/Test environment with transport release mechanism followed.

 

**Additional information –

Use the Fiori Application Extensibility Inventory in both the S/4 HANA Cloud versions to check dependencies of objects before transporting.

entry.title: WIP Revaluation in SAP Actual Costing (S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)
entry.content:
This blog answers the following questions:

  • Is WIP revaluation relevant to your business?
  • How to ensure WIP is revaluated properly in Actual Costing?
  • What are WIP buildup and WIP reduction?
  • Example
  • How is WIP in Actual Costing different from WIP in Production Accounting?
  • Conclusion

Is WIP revaluation relevant to your business?

In a manufacturing business, it’s easy to focus only on the finished products rolling off the line. But there’s something else just as important: Work in process (WIP) - the unfinished items scattered across the production floor. The material components and the labor costs tied up in these in‑progress products all carry real value.

Before these items become finished goods and eventually show up as part of the cost of goods sold (COGS), their value is a key component of the balance sheet. Without Actual Costing, WIP materials and activities are valued at planned price (standard price). Similar to what is described in blog: Actual Costing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for revaluation of COGS and inventory, SAP Actual Costing revaluates WIP at actual price and ensures higher accuracy in legal and management reports.

In summary, WIP revaluation with SAP Actual Costing is relevant if:

  • You run a manufacturing business.
  • Your business has a significant amount of WIP on the production line at period end.
  • The prices of materials and activities consumed for production tend to fluctuate throughout the period.
  • You want to valuate these WIP materials and activities at actual price rather than standard price.

How to ensure WIP is revaluated properly in Actual Costing?

In Actual Costing, WIP is revaluated as part of consumption in Settlement step in actual costing runs (CKMLCP).

  • For materials, the price differences collected from receipts in the period are allocated to consumption and ending inventory proportionally by quantity.
  • Production activities are also revaluated based on consumed quantities and actual cost rates maintained in Manage Cost Rates – Actual (F3422) or Actual Cost Rate Calculation - Cost Centers (KSII).

As you can see, quantity calculation is crucial to ensure correct WIP revaluation. Therefore, before executing actual costing run (in CKMLCP), remember to let the system know the WIP quantities of materials and activities by executing the job “WIP Quantity Calculation for Actual Costing: Event-Based Order and PCC” in app Schedule Inventory Accounting Jobs (F2292).

For any materials or activities consumed in a production order, the system determines WIP quantities with the formula:

WIP quantity = Actual consumption - Target consumption, that is:

WIP quantity = Actual consumption quantity - Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

If the calculated WIP quantity for a material or activity is negative, the system recognizes zero WIP quantity. No allocation to WIP will occur for it.

What are WIP buildup and WIP reduction?

As often seen in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N), “WIP Buildup” and “WIP Reduction” refer to the following:

  • WIP Build Up for Order <order ID>: Increase of WIP quantity in the current period.
  • WIP Reduction for Order <order ID> <previous period>: Reverse partial or full WIP quantity generated in previous period. This part of WIP quantity has been transformed into finished products and delivered to stock in current period.

Note: This reduction is based on a FIFO (First-In, First-Out) principle, meaning the oldest WIP layer is reduced first.

After the Settlement step in costing run (CKMLCP), the following new lines show up for above WIP quantity changes:

  • Revaluation of WIP Build Up <current period>: The price difference allocated to the WIP quantity increase in current period.
  • Revaluation of WIP Reduction <previous period>: The price difference allocated to the WIP quantity that is cumulated from previous period but is consumed into finished products in the current period.

Example

The following example shows how WIP is accumulated and reduced over a 3‑period production process, and how it is revaluated in each period.

Planned Material Components and Activities in Order

A production order (1011600) is created for manufacturing 100 PC of FG228 in period 2025/009. The planned input materials and activities are as follows:

Table 1: Planned Input Materials and Activities

Material/ActivityQuantity
RM16100 PC
RM27100 PC
Activity 1 (Machine)800 min (13.333 H)
Activity 3 (setup)120 min (2 H)
Activity 11 (Personnel)800 min (13.333 H)

You can also view the planned quantities in order itemization below:

Planned Material and Activity Consumption of Order 1011600

Material/Activity Consumption and Partial Delivery in Period 009

In period 2025/009, you withdraw materials and confirm activities for the production, and deliver finished products to stock as follows:

Table 2: Order Transactions in Period 009

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Target Consumption of Material/Activity

Delivery to Stock

009

RM16

60 PC

100 PC * (50 PC/100 PC) = 50 PC

/

 

RM27

40 PC

100 PC * (50 PC/100 PC) = 50 PC

/

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

300 min (5 H)

800 min * (50 PC/100 PC) = 6.667 H

/

 

Activity 3 (setup)

90 min (1.5 H)

2 H * (50 PC/100 PC) = 1 H

/

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

300 min (5 H)

800 min * (50 PC/100 PC) = 6.667 H

/

 

FG228

/

 

50 PC

Target consumption is calculated using the formula:

Target consumption = Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

You can verify the above actual quantities and target quantities in Order Cost Details – Event-Based (F4254):

Quantity and Cost Details of Order

Please ignore the rounding differences in minute-to-hour conversion. Also, activity 3 (Setup) is a lot-size-independent activity, hence the target quantity 2H (equal to plan quantity).

Calculate WIP Quantity of Production Order in Period 009

At period end, you run the job “WIP Quantity Calculation for Actual Costing: Event-Based Order and PCC” in F2292:

WIP Quantity Calculation Job

 The calculated WIP quantities can be viewed in Display WIP Quantities Document (COMLWIPDOC):

WIP Quantity Calculation Result of Period 009

 The system determines 10 PC of raw material RM16 and 0.5 hour of activity 3 (Setup) as WIP for the order 1011600 using the formula:

WIP quantity = Actual Consumption - Target consumption, i.e.

WIP quantity = Actual consumption quantity - Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

Table 3: How WIP quantities are calculated for period 009?

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption

Target Consumption (See table 2 for calculation)

WIP Quantity

009

RM16

60 PC

50 PC

60 PC – 50 PC = 10 PC

 

RM27

40 PC

50 PC

40 PC – 50 PC = -10 PC (negative). No WIP.

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

300 min (5 H)

6.667 H

Negative, no WIP

 

Activity 3 (setup)

90 min (1.5 H)

1 H

1.5 H - 1H = 0.5H

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

300 min (5 H)

6.667 H

Negative, no WIP

View Actual Costing Settlement Result

Unlike other materials or activities, RM16 and activity 3 were partially consumed for completed production and partially for WIP production. Therefore, at the period end, when you settle their price differences in the actual costing run (CKMLCP), the price differences are allocated to “Production of FG228” and “WIP Build Up” in proportion of quantity as shown below:

RM16 WIP and Production Consumption in Period 009

 Activity 3 WIP and Production Consumption in Period 009

 As shown in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N) for the finished product FG228, only the price differences of non-WIP parts of RM16 and activity 3 are rolled up.

Quantity Structure of Product FG228 in Period 009

View Posted Revaluation Journal Entries

After executing Post Closing in actual costing run (CKMLCP), the following accounting journal entries are posted by Actual Costing. Both include the WIP revaluation lines.

WIP of RM16 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 009)

 Journal entry 4700000057 is for all materials involved in the costing run:

  • Line 19: PRY (Price Difference - Material)

The price difference of 77.22 EUR is received for material RM16 and will be transferred to its WIP account.

  • Line 20: WPM (WIP from Price Difference - Material)

The price difference is debited to WIP account in order to bring the total WIP value in balance sheet to actual cost.

Activity 3 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 009)

Journal entry 4700000059 is for all activities involved in the costing run:

  • Line 14: PRL (Price Difference - Activity)

The price difference of 0.21 EUR is received for activity 3, e.g. due to deviation between actual and planned cost rate of the activity. It will be transferred to the WIP account.

  • Line 15: WPA (WIP from Price Difference - Activity)

The price difference is debited to WIP account to bring the total WIP value in balance sheet to actual cost.

With that, you can ensure the balance sheet (and P&L) are updated with the real WIP values in period 009.     

More Material/Activity Consumption and Delivery in Period 010

Production continues in period 010. More activities/materials are consumed and finished products delivered:

Table 4: Order Transactions in Period 010

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Delivery to Stock

010

RM16

30 PC

/

 

RM27

50 PC

/

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

/

/

 

Activity 3 (setup)

/

/

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

480 min (8 H)

/

 

FG228

/

30 PC

Calculate WIP Quantity of Production Order in Period 010

Till the end of 010, the product FG228 delivered to stock has increased to 80 PC. The new delivery and consumption in period 0101 change the WIP quantity of raw materials and activities to the following:

Table 5: How WIP quantities are calculated for period 010?

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Target Consumption of Material/Activity

Total WIP Quantity at End of Period 10

009 + 010

RM16

90 PC

100 PC * (80 PC/100 PC) = 80 PC

90 PC – 80 PC = 10 PC

 

RM27

90 PC

100 PC * (80 PC/100 PC) = 80 PC

90 PC – 80 PC = 10 PC

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

300 min (5 H)

800 min * (80 PC/100 PC) = 10.665 H

Negative, no WIP

 

Activity 3 (setup)

90 min (1.5 H)

2 H * (80 PC/100 PC) = 1.6 H

Negative, no WIP

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

780 min (13 H)

800 min * (80 PC/100 PC) = 10.665 H

13 H - 10.665 H = 2.335 H

As we have WIP quantities (of RM16 and activity 3) in period 009, according to the FIFO principle, in the new period 010, these quantities should be consumed first, then followed by the new materials withdrawn and activities confirmed in period 010. Therefore, after you run the WIP quantity calculation job, the WIP quantity by period is updated to the following:

WIP Quantity Calculation Result of Period 010

 Calculation of WIP changes of both periods is detailed in the table below:

Table 6: WIP Quantity Changes of Period 009 and 010

Material/Activity

Current Total WIP Quantity

WIP Quantity at Period 9

WIP Change of Period 9

WIP Increase of Period 10

RM16

10 PC

10 PC

- 10 PC (WIP Reduction)

10 PC

RM27

10 PC

0

0 (No change)

10 PC

Activity 1 (Machine)

0

0

0 (No change)

0

Activity 3 (setup)

0

0.5H

-0.5 H (WIP Reduction)

0

Activity 11 (Personnel)

2.335 H

0

0 (No change)

2.335 H (WIP Buildup)

View Quantity Structure of FG228 of Period 010

The quantity structure of FG228 of period 010 indicates: to manufacture 30 PC of FG228 in period 010, how many quantities of materials and activities were actually consumed.

WIP Reduction

The 10 PC of RM16 and 0.5 H of activity 3 remaining in WIP accounts of period 009 are consumed in period 010, hence the WIP Reduction as highlighted below:

WIP Reduction Shown in Quantity Structure of FG228 (Period 010)

Important: Although these 2 WIP reduction lines are consumed for manufacturing F228 in period 010, their costs are calculated based on the determined price of period 009:

  • RM16 at price 7.97 EUR/PC of period 009 (in comparison, RM16 has the price 0.25 EUR/PC in period 010 as shown in the line above)
  • Activity 3 at price 8.50 EUR/hour of period 009
WIP Buildup and Consumption to Production

Besides the above highlighted WIP reduction, the new consumption in period 010 is divided into WIP buildup and production consumption as follows:

Table 7: WIP Buildup and Consumption to Production

Material/Activity

New Consumption in Period 010

 

WIP Quantities of Period 010 (as in table 5)

 

Consumed for Production of 30 PC FG228 in Period 010 (as in Qty Structure)

RM16

30 PC

=

10 PC

+

20 PC

RM27

50 PC

=

10 PC

+

40 PC

Activity 11 (Personnel)

8 H

=

2.335 H (WIP Buildup)

+

5.665 H

As shown in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N) of RM16 for period 010, the new consumption of 30 PC is divided into 20 PC of production consumption and 10 PC of WIP buildup (if you click button WIP, the WIP Reduction of Period 009 line can show up under Production).

WIP and Production Consumption of RM16 in Period 010

View Accounting Journal Entries of Period 010

After executing the Post Closing step in actual costing run (CKMLCP), we can check the accounting journal entries posted for WIP items.

WIP Reduction and buildup of RM16

Since RM16 has both WIP reduction and WIP buildup, we can take it as an example to examine the WIP-related journal entry lines:

WIP Reduction and Buildup of RM16 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 010)

  • Pair of lines 17 and 18: Price difference of 77.22 EUR (PRY) is passed onto WIP increase/buildup (WPM debit) of period 010).

Note: This is similar to lines 25 and 26 for RM27, representing WIP increase in one period.

  • Pair of lines 19 and 20: Remaining WIP difference of 77.22 EUR of previous period 009 is deducted (WPM credit) in period 010 and offset by material WIP reduction (PRM).

Note: PRM is only relevant in cross-period WIP reduction.

WIP Reduction of Activity 3

Activity 3 only has a WIP reduction of 0.5 hours in period 010. As shown in the journal entry below, the cumulated WIP price difference of 0.21 EUR is decreased (WPA credit) and offset by activity WIP reduction (PRA).

WIP Reduction of Activity 3 in Accounting Journal Entry (Period 010)

Material Consumption and Final Delivery in Period 011

In period 011, more materials are withdrawn, and more products are delivered as below:

Table 8: Order Transactions in Period 011

Period

Material/Activity

Actual Consumption of Material/Activity

Delivery to Stock

011

RM16

35 PC

/

 

RM27

/

/

 

Activity 1 (Machine)

/

/

 

Activity 3 (setup)

/

/

 

Activity 11 (Personnel)

/

/

 

FG228

/

15 PC

The total delivery quantity reaches 95 PC (50 + 30 + 15) and we decide to set the production order to Technically Completed (TECO).  

With that, all remaining WIP from previous period 010 are consumed for the production of 15 PC in period 011. After we execute the WIP quantity calculation job, all WIP quantities of period 010 are cleared up. You can cross-check the WIP quantities with table 6.

WIP Quantity Calculation Result for Period 011

Taking RM16 as an example, quantities and values under WIP Production are cleared and moved to under Production in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N).

WIP Quantity Moved to Production for RM16 as Order is Completed

View Quantity Structure of FG228 of Period 011

As seen from the valuated quantity structure of FG228 in period 011, to produce 15 PC of FG228, all remaining WIP materials and activities from period 010, along with the new withdrawal of 35 PC of RM16 in period 011, are consumed.

WIP Reduction of Materials/Activities Shown in Quantity Structure of FG228 (Period 011)

View Accounting Journal Entries of Period 011

With the completion of Post Closing step, remaining WIP of RM16 from previous period 010 (WPM) is passed onto its WIP reduction (PRM), which is in turn, rolled up to product FG228 (PRY).

WIP Reduction of RM16 and Difference Rollup to FG228

That concludes our example of cross-period WIP buildup and reduction.

How is WIP in Actual Costing different from WIP in Production Accounting?

Working in manufacturing, you’re probably quite familiar with WIP value of production orders. You might wonder how it is different or related to the WIP revaluation we explained above. It can be summarized as follows:

Table 9: Comparison Between WIP in Production Accounting and WIP in Actual Costing

 

WIP in Production Accounting

WIP Revaluation in Actual Costing

Valuated Object

Production order, process order, product cost collector

Materials/activities consumed for production in a period.

Note: Materials must have a price determination 3 (Single/Multi-level).

Definition

Value of unfinished products

Value difference of consumed materials/activities that haven’t resulted in finished products

Calculation Method

Difference between debit and credit of an order

WIP quantity multiplied by price difference

Calculation Formula

WIP value = Actual cost (debit) - Goods receipt value (credit), that is:

WIP = Standard material cost + standard activity cost + overhead cost - goods receipt value of manufactured product

Note: All materials, activities, and products are valuated at standard price.

Note: Calculation for product cost collectors is slightly different than above. See https://help.sap.com/s4_ce_prd_acc.

WIP revaluation value of each material/activity = WIP quantity * (actual price - standard price), where “WIP quantity” is calculated based on all orders of the period with the formula:

WIP quantity = Actual consumption quantity - Planned consumption quantity * (actual delivery quantity/planned delivery quantity)

Calculation Timepoint

Automatic and event-based (whenever goods movement or activity confirmation is performed)

WIP quantity calculation is manually triggered by job “WIP Quantity Calculation for Actual Costing: Event-Based Order and PCC” in F2292.

WIP revaluation is done with Settlement step in actual costing run (CKMLCP).

Period 009 Example

WIP value is:

-1120.81 EUR = (goods issue of 45 EUR + activity confirmation of 391.86 EUR + overhead of 42.33 EUR) – goods receipt of 1600 EUR.

Note: In the accounting journal entry posted by Production Accounting, the value is posted with the following dimensions:

  • Order 1011600
  • Product FG228
  • G/L account: Configured in SSCUI Event-Based WIP and Variance Posting (103940)
  • Reference Transaction: EBWP

WIP revaluation of RM16 is:

77.22 EUR = WIP quantity of 10 PC * (actual price of 7.97 EUR – standard price of 0.25 EUR).

Note: In the accounting journal entry posted by Actual Costing, the value is posted with the following dimensions

  • Order: Empty (sum of all orders in the period)
  • Product: RM16
  • G/L account: Configured for transaction key WPM in SSCUI Automatic Account Determination (100297)
  • Reference Transaction: MLHD

WIP revaluation of activity 3 is:

0.21 EUR = WIP quantity of 0.5 H * (actual cost rate of 8.50 EUR – planned cost rate of 8.08 EUR).

Note: In the accounting journal entry posted by Actual Costing, the value is posted with the following dimensions

  • Order: Empty (sum of all orders in the period)
  • Product: Empty
  • Activity: Empty
  • G/L account: Configured for transaction key WPA in SSCUI Automatic Account Determination (100297)
  • Reference Transaction: MLHD

The following screens are provided for you to verify the WIP calculation explained in the above table:

Cost Details of Order 1011600 in Period

The above screen from Order Cost Details – Event Based (F4254) shows the business transactions occur on the production order 1011600 till end of period 009. Based on that, the WIP value (-1120.81 EUR) is determined by summing up all incurred costs and then deducting the goods receipt value.

WIP Revaluation of RM16 in Period 009

 The above screen shows in Material Price Analysis (CKM3N), the WIP revaluated for RM16 is 77.22 EUR. It’s determined by multiplying the WIP quantity (10 PC) by the difference between actual price (7.97 EUR) and standard price (0.25 EUR) of RM16.

Note: Actual price of RM16 is only available after executing Settlement step in Actual Costing. Before that, only the standard WIP value (2.50 EUR = 10 PC * 0.25 EUR/PC) is available, as shown in Preliminary Valuation (PrelimVal) column. WIP Revaluation of Activity 3 in Period 009

Similarly, the above screen shows in Activity Consumption Analysis (CKM3A), the WIP revaluated for activity 3 is 0.21 EUR. It’s determined by multiplying the WIP quantity (0.5 H) by the difference between actual cost rate (8.50 EUR) and planned cost rate (8.08 EUR) of activity 3.

Note: Actual cost rate is read from F3422 or KSII, whichever is the latest. The Settlement step of Actual Costing revaluates the WIP.

In summary, Production Accounting calculates standard WIP value for individual orders, while Actual Costing revaluates the WIP by using the actual price/cost rate on material/activity level. By combining both, you can achieve a more accurate WIP value, and a better view of how well your production and inventory operations are running.

Conclusion

This blog explores when WIP matters for your business and how SAP Actual Costing revalues it with a cross‑period production example. By understanding the mechanics behind WIP calculation and revaluation, you can turn cost transparency into a real operational advantage.

entry.title: Announcing: Enhancements to Quality Gates and Embedded Launch Activities for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM
entry.content:
Enhancing Project Success: New CQC Reports and Embedded Launch Activity support for Quality Gates

There's a lot that needs to be done, done correctly, and done at the right time for your SAP solution deployment to go smoothly. SAP created the SAP Activate Methodology to guide project teams through the most essential tasks for successful implementation, but how can you check that you're on track as you go? This is what quality gates are designed to do. Quality gates are periodic checkpoints that focus in on the most essential tasks that should be completed before moving onto the next phase. Multiple teams in SAP have come together in 2025 to improve the quality gates, and in this blog I go through the 3 major improvements to Quality Gates in HCM and SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition). 

  1. Refreshed Quality Gate Content
  2. Automated Risk Analysis via CQC Reports in SAP Cloud ALM
  3. 1:1 Quality Gate Support available via Embedded Launch Activities

Quality gate execution is a collaborative milestone, driven by a joint effort between partner and customer project teams and led by their respective project managers. These checkpoints empower teams to demonstrate systematic due diligence and clear, tangible progress toward solution adoption and realized business value.

See the sections below for details on each of these enhancements.

1. Refreshed Quality Gate Content

All quality gate questions for all SAP Activate phases have been reviewed and refreshed. Key improvements are:

  1. All checklist questions have been refreshed to be applicable to the latest SAP concepts, products, and assets
  2. Relevant supporting links and assets have been added to the question documentation (e.g. explanatory links of what the Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) is have been linked in checklist questions that address the DDA)
  3. New supporting assets created to articulate the concept of quality gates, why they're important, and the CQC reports now available (Quality Gate Overview Deck, Quality Gate Execution - Detailed Walkthrough Deck, SAP Activate & Quality Gate Introductory Video)
  4. Language has been harmonized across the business suite - i.e. roles and terms will be more consistent across roadmaps from different solution areas such as HCM, ERP (Public and Private), Concur, SCM, BTP etc.

The best way to access and review Quality Gate Questions is via SAP Cloud ALM, but you can also view the refreshed list of questions via the SAP Activate Roadmaps accelerators linked here (HCM | SAP Cloud ERP)

2. Automated Risk Analysis: The CQC Report for Quality Gates

The biggest hurdle in a Quality Gate isn't just answering the questions - it's knowing what to do when an answer reveals a risk.

When you complete your Quality Gate checklists directly in SAP Cloud ALM, you can now generate a Continuous Quality Check (CQC) for Implementation Gates report*. This is a tailored advisory document generated within one business day.

*As of 2026-02, this report is available to request in SAP Cloud ALM for the Prepare and Explore Phase Quality Gates for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM. It will be rolled out for Realize, Deploy and Run in Q2 2026 for SAP Cloud ERP and HCM. It is already available for all phases for SAP Cloud ERP Private.  Availability for other solution areas is under development for 2026.

What the CQC Report delivers:

  • Management Summary: A high-level view for stakeholders on project readiness.

  • Individualized Action Plan: Trackable tasks in SAP Cloud ALM to course-correct identified risks.

  • Detailed Recommendations: Direct links to SAP accelerators and documentation to help your team resolve gaps.

By using this self-service report at the end of the Prepare and Explore phases, you mitigate early-stage risks like unvalidated financial structures or incomplete integration designs before they impact your delivery timeline.

3. Personalized Guidance: Enhanced Embedded Launch Activities

We know that digital tools are most effective when paired with expert human insight. That’s why we are enhancing our Embedded Launch Activities - the onboarding services many of you already utilize.

Starting with new customer contracts as of March 1, 2026 we are including 1:1 Onboarding Advisor support for one of your Quality Gate checklists. This means you can have a dedicated SAP expert walk through the checklist with you. The onboarding advisor will be able to support with on the spot clarifications of concepts, resources etc.,  helping the team to ensure that your "Yes" answers are truly backed by best practices and that your "No" answers have a solid mitigation plan in place.

Why This Matters for Your Project

Quality Gates occur at every major transition point - from the end of Prepare through to Run (where they repeat every 6 months). They are designed to be "health checks," not performance reviews.

By utilizing the new CQC reports and Advisor support, you achieve two primary things:

  1. Confidence: That the project team has completed the most essential tasks, is aware of the most essential assets, and knows what you need to know to move forward, or course correct.

  2. Speed: You're more likely to achieve your goals on time by doing it right the first time - and the checklists help to safeguard that.

     

Next Steps: How to Get Started

  • Access your project in SAP Cloud ALM: Ensure your Quality Gate checklists are being completed digitally rather than in manual spreadsheets to unlock report functionality. Follow this detailed guide to begin utilizing quality gates in SAP Cloud ALM.

  • Request your CQC Report: For SAP Cloud ERP and HCM projects for Prepare & Explore Phases, once the checklist is complete, use the “Request SAP Assessment Service” button. Your report will be available in SAP Cloud ALM and SAP for Me within 24 hours.

  • Book your Advisor Session: For new customer contracts as of March 1st, reach out to your onboarding advisor to schedule your 1:1 session for your chosen Quality Gate milestone.  

Let us know your thoughts or questions in the comments below!

entry.title: Migration Cockpit in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Welcome to your one-stop shop to learn and share knowledge about SAP's ready-to-run public cloud ERP software solution.  

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

Migration Cockpit

  • Explore Migration Cockpit
  • Using Staging Tables as Intermediate Storage
  • Transfer List
  • Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

Explore Migration Objects: A New SAP Fiori App for Enhanced Project Planning

A successful data migration relies on meticulous planning and a deep understanding of data dependencies. With the 2602 release, we are introducing the SAP Fiori app, "Explore Migration Objects," to bring a new level of clarity to your project preparation.

This app provides crucial insights into migration object dependencies and their relationships to specific scope items. It empowers your project team to efficiently plan data migration projects by ensuring that all mandatory and optional predecessor objects are correctly identified and migrated in the proper sequence. Through interactive graphs, you can now visualize how migration objects are interconnected. The app allows for detailed dependency analysis, differentiating between mandatory and optional predecessors, and provides focused search and direct access to migration object documentation. By visualizing these relationships, your team can make informed decisions, ultimately reducing project risks and optimizing timelines. Please note that this initial version of the app currently supports migration objects built for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach.

 

 

Enhanced Flexibility with Staging Tables for Direct System Migration

For customers using the "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System" approach, the 2602 release introduces a powerful new capability: the use of staging tables as intermediate storage. This feature is designed to facilitate the adjustment of selected data before it is loaded into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

This enhancement significantly increases transparency and provides robust validation capabilities for your selected data. It gives your team more flexibility to adjust and cleanse application data from the source SAP system before the Now, data adaptation is performed within ETL tool prior to loading the data into the migration cockpit’s staging tables. Once the data is prepared and validated, you can transfer the ready-for-processing data to the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit for further processing, ensuring higher data quality in your new cloud environment.

 

 

 

Streamline Your Migration Troubleshooting with the New Transfer List

Transfer List

In any complex data migration project, ensuring data is transferred accurately is paramount. When issues arise during the simulation or migration phases, pinpointing the root cause can be time-consuming. To bring greater transparency to this process, we are introducing the Transfer List for the migration approach "Migrate Data Directly from SAP System." This feature is designed for data migration specialists and project teams who need deep visibility into the data transfer process for effective and efficient troubleshooting.


A Clear View for Faster Resolution

The Transfer List provides a detailed view of the data exactly as it is passed to the target API that creates the records in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
This capability is particularly beneficial when you need to troubleshoot the simulation or migration process. For example, your team can now analyze the Transfer List to definitively determine whether an issue lies with the target API itself or if it is related to a problem in the structure or field mapping. This clarity helps you isolate problems faster, reduce resolution time, and keep your migration project on track.

To use this feature, the simulation or migration process must first be completed for the relevant migration object instance. Once complete, you can access the Transfer List with the following steps:

1. From the Migration Project screen, select the "Instances" link for your object.
2. On the Migration Object Instances screen, select the instance you wish to investigate.
3. Choose the "Transfer List" button.

On the subsequent screen, you can view the target parameters, fields, and their corresponding values that were used in the transfer, providing your team with the precise information needed for analysis. This enhancement empowers you to resolve issues with confidence and precision, ensuring a smoother transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Increased File Size Limits for an Efficient Data Load

To further streamline your data migration activities, we have increased the file size limits for the "Migrate Data Using Staging Tables" approach. This practical update allows for more efficient handling of large data volumes.

When you upload your data using XML files, you can bundle multiple files into a single zip file for a more convenient upload. With this release, the maximum size for each individual XML file contained within the zip file has been increased to 400MB. This allows you to process larger data sets in a single file. Please be aware that while individual files can be larger, the total size of the zip file itself should not exceed 100MB upon upload to the migration cockpit. This improvement simplifies the data loading process and reduces the number of uploads required for extensive data sets.

I hope you're as excited about these updates as I am! To learn more, I encourage you to explore the SAP Help Portal and join the conversation on the SAP Community.

What is New?

Check Migration Cockpit Community

SAP Help Data Migration

SAP Community YouTube presented by Claudia Gemm and Elizaveta Kozhevenikova

  

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for Migration Cockpit as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:
